pages.full_text:
Matt + the children are very good company - Matt smokes delicious-smelling cigars. There are several games of cricket with other children on the campsite too.
    On Saturday we go to the arts crafts village which has a very good acquarium, lobsters, octopi, trout, crabs. There is also a museum with sights + sounds of yesteryear - a blacksmiths forge, a carpenter /coffin-maker / stores. The RAF was stationed at Barmouth in WWII, so there's an aircraft museum too , + a children's playground.
    We returned to the van for lunch - the Portals perched on the back bumper with the doors hinged all the way back for the first time.
pages.notes: Page continues from a previous spread about a stay near Barmouth. 'acquarium' is diary's own spelling. 'blacksmiths' appears without apostrophe in the original. The word 'arts' and 'crafts' appear on consecutive lines with a line break between them (arts / crafts village).

pages.full_text:
The Portals have left this afternoon, + Eve has been napping in the van with John + Charles while Simon takes Thomas, William + Peter on a very long walk - 2pm - 6 pm ? To be followed by a barbque?
    As well as the tent, we have added other supplies to the camping van this summer - a cutlery drainer, changing the double sleeping bag into 2 duvet-style quilts + fitted bottom sheet. Simon has enlarged the entrance to some of the cupboards + built a box to hold maps on his door. We now have 3 small grey bags for dirty clothes. Charles has a specially notified cardboard box to travel in so that he can't climb about.
pages.notes: The word 'double' is written as a small interlinear annotation above 'sleeping' in 'changing the double sleeping bag into 2 duvet-style quilts'. 'barbque' is diary's spelling (abbreviation of barbecue). 'notified' may be a misread — could read 'modified'; best reading given as 'notified' but 'modified' is the likely intended word.
